label: warned about MP-2 fraud
description: Shipione raised concerns to city council members about the underfunding proposal in 2002, calling it 'possibly corrupt.' Her warning was ignored by all members except Donna Frye.

label: alerted mayor to underfunding scheme
description: Shipione raised concerns about the pension underfunding proposal to Mayor Murphy's administration. Her warnings were not acted upon.

label: designed underfunding structure
description: Uberuaga, as City Manager, inherited and expanded the MP-1 underfunding framework, establishing the structural mechanism that set the stage for MP-2.

label: designed and proposed MP-2
description: Uberuaga as City Manager designed Manager's Proposal 2 and brought it to the pension board and city council for approval.

label: cast decisive vote; received special benefit
description: Saathoff voted to approve MP-2 on the pension board. Federal prosecutors alleged his vote was secured through a quid pro quo: a special pension benefit allowing combined salary calculation, worth ~$25,000/year more.
notes: Classified as 'alleged' because federal charges were dismissed. The vote is verified; the quid pro quo framing is the prosecution's theory.

label: designed special pension benefit
description: Lexin was accused of designing the special pension benefit for Saathoff that allowed him to combine salaries when calculating his pension, increasing it by ~$25,000/year. Federal charges dismissed 2010.

label: co-devised special pension benefit
description: Grissom, as SDCERS administrator, was accused of devising the special benefit for Saathoff in coordination with Lexin, Webster, and Chapin.

label: designed and concealed special benefit
description: Chapin, as SDCERS chief pension attorney, was accused of designing and legally concealing the special pension benefit for Saathoff.

label: sole dissenting vote
description: Only city council member to vote NO on the November 18, 2002 pension benefit increases after being warned by Diann Shipione.

label: approved as council president
description: Peters, as city council president, voted to approve the MP-2 benefit increases. Later argued he did not originate the plan.
notes: Investigators later characterized his role as 'negligent' rather than criminally culpable.

label: signed false bond certifications
description: Ryan signed letters falsely representing that the city's audited financials in the bond offerings were accurate, omitting the pension underfunding crisis.

label: drafted false bond disclosures
description: Vattimo participated in drafting the misleading bond offering documents that omitted the pension funding crisis.

label: revised and signed false disclosures
description: Frazier revised the bond disclosure documents and signed closing letters for two bond offerings that omitted material pension information.

label: issued unqualified audits despite omissions
description: CJO issued unqualified audit reports on bond offerings despite failing to comply with GAAS and failing to identify the material omissions about pension obligations.

label: pension omissions triggered rating withdrawal
description: The January 2004 public disclosure of pension omissions led S&P to withdraw the city's AA-minus bond rating by September 2004.

label: resigned amid scandal
description: Murphy announced his resignation on April 25, 2005, directly attributable to the pension scandal and federal investigations into the city.

label: succeeded as mayor
description: Sanders was elected in the November 2005 special election to succeed Murphy and inherited the pension crisis.

label: filed state felony charges
description: DA Dumanis filed state felony charges under California Gov Code 1090 against six officials for conflict-of-interest in the pension benefit deals. Lexin was among the primary defendants.

label: filed federal fraud indictments
description: U.S. Attorney's Office secured federal grand jury indictments against five pension officials on fraud and conspiracy charges related to the Saathoff benefit quid pro quo.

label: issued securities fraud order against city
description: SEC formally sanctioned the City of San Diego for securities fraud in municipal bond offerings that raised $260 million based on false and misleading disclosures.

label: charged independent auditor with fraud
description: SEC charged Thomas Saiz and CJO with fraud for issuing unqualified audit reports that enabled the false bond disclosures.

label: charged five officials with securities fraud
description: SEC charged Uberuaga, Ryan, Frazier, Vattimo, and Webster with securities fraud for participating in drafting and certifying false bond disclosures.

label: settled fraud charges
description: Four of five SEC defendants settled their charges in October 2010, paying a combined $80,000 in penalties without admitting or denying wrongdoing.

label: dismissed — statute unconstitutionally vague
description: All federal criminal charges against the five pension officials were dismissed when Judge Benitez ruled the honest services fraud statute was unconstitutionally vague.

label: reinstated bond rating at A
description: S&P's 2008 reinstatement ended four years of bond market exclusion, but at a lower rating (A vs. former AA-minus).

label: union president
description: Saathoff was president of Firefighters Union Local 145 while simultaneously serving as a pension board trustee — the dual role at the heart of the conflict-of-interest.

label: principal of firm
description: Saiz was the principal responsible for conducting the city audits at CJO.

label: attempted to overturn illegal benefits
description: City Attorney Aguirre took control of SDCERS's legal affairs in December 2005, seized documents, and attempted to invalidate $900M in pension benefit increases, ultimately without success.

label: four-year bond market exclusion
description: S&P's 2004 rating withdrawal effectively locked San Diego out of the bond market for four years.
